Wiki / Shops & upgrades

Shops, staff & upgrades

Turn click income into a self-running operation: hire staff, build out your shop, train your crew, and stack upgrades across six categories.

Passive income

Employees & shop extensions

Around 37 shop & staff types live in the Shops tab: 14 employee tiers that generate passive income, plus shop extensions and connectivity that add income and global multipliers. Shops unlock at Level 3, employees at Level 5.

TierEmployeeBase income
1Student Interns$0.3 / s
2Skilled Baristas$1.2 / s
3Trained Chefs$4 / s
4Shift Supervisors$15 / s
5Location Managers$65 / s
6Regional Directors$190 / s
7National Operations$650 / s
8Distribution Centers$2.5K / s
9Global Supply Chain$9.5K / s
10Corporate HQ$35K / s
11Brand Ambassadors$95K / s
12Franchise Network$420K / s
13–14Global Hub → Galactic Operationsmillions / s

Shop extensions & connectivity — indoor seating, kitchen overhauls, drive-throughs, WiFi networks, and automation systems — add their own income and, in the case of WiFi/tech, a global multiplier on top of everything else. Each employee tier also adds to your monthly payroll.

Crew

Staff training & wages

Staff training

Train each employee type up to Level 3, paid with XP and gated by how many of that employee you own. Each level adds +20% income to that type (so Level 3 = +60%).

Level 1≥10 owned · 100 XP · +20%
Level 2≥25 owned · 250 XP · +40%
Level 3≥50 owned · 500 XP · +60%

Wage management

Unlocks at Level 10. Set wages to trade payroll cost against staff performance — lower wages save money but hurt your customer rating and speed; higher wages do the reverse.

Monthly payroll and a base 35% tax are deducted periodically; Hiring Pipeline and Tax Optimization research soften both.

Prices & offline income

Shake price control (Level 7) lets you set your selling price — charge more per shake at some cost to your customer rating, or keep prices friendly. The Shift Manager upgrade earns offline income while you're away.

Power spikes

40 upgrades

Upgrades come in six categories. Most are tiered (buy multiple levels at escalating cost); a handful are powerful one-time purchases.

Production

Mix Speed, Portion Size, Equipment, Custom Blending, Auto-Mixer Tuning, Extra Blender, Speed Blending.

Quality

Golden Touch, Quality Control, Special Mastery, Ingredient Quality, Master Mixologist, Premium Packaging.

Management

Employee Training, Talent Recruitment, Distribution Network, Shift Manager, Rush-Hour Optimization, Location Slots.

Sales

Marketing Campaign, Storefront Appeal, Brand Licensing, Loyalty Program, Social-Media Buzz, Viral Marketing AI.

Operations

Bulk Purchasing, Expansion Negotiation, WiFi Optimization, Cloud-Kitchen Integration, Supply-Chain Optimization, Freezer Tech.

Research

Recipe Development, Customer Analytics AI, Quantum Logistics — high-impact compounding multipliers.

Special one-time upgrades (Level 8+) are game-changers: Golden Touch (×5 golden chance), Double Shot (2× manual income), Speed Blending (halve blend time), Flavor Slots (up to 5 active flavors), Extra Blender (up to 4 blenders), and Location Slots (more locations).

Buying

Bulk buying & discounts

A quantity selector lets you buy in batches of 1 / 5 / 10 / Max, with a bulk discount that grows with batch size.

QuantityBulk discount
×1none
×5~2% per item
×10~5% per item
×Max (50+)~10% per item

Stack that with the Bulk Purchasing upgrade (−5% per level) and Bulk Procurement research for deeper discounts, plus Hiring Pipeline research for cheaper employees specifically.

What's new

New in v1.3

v1.3 rebalances the income curve, introduces an ingredients economy, and adds structured employee roles to the Employees tab.

  • ~11% slower income — all manual and passive income is reduced by a gentle global pacing nudge (profit divisor 2.5 → 2.8) to lengthen the curve without making the early game feel sluggish.
  • Monthly tax raised to 0.42 (was 0.35), so a growing empire carries real ongoing upkeep.
  • Ingredients & supply chain — every blend now consumes supplies. See the new Ingredients & supply chain page for the full dashboard, drain rates, restocking, and the hard-stop rule.
  • Employee roles — hired staff can be split into Production, Management, and Restock roles, with an optional Auto-Assign toggle that keeps the split balanced as headcount changes. See the new Employee roles page for details.
